summary.intent: Run a comprehensive 5-agent parallel code review of the optimize-anything project, then autonomously fix all identified issues using a ralph-loop to bring the repo to marketplace-ready state.
summary.outcome: All 10 critical and 15 moderate issues resolved across 4 batches of commits; test count grew from 252 to 258; 5 commits pushed to main covering LICENSE, 6 missing command stubs, 4 code correctness fixes, 6 new tests, and 4 documentation accuracy fixes.

repo: Plugin had a manifest/filesystem mismatch: plugin.json declared 7 slash commands but only commands/optimize.md existed; Claude Code discovers commands from commands/*.md files, not the manifest array
No LICENSE file existed despite pyproject.toml declaring `license = {text = 'MIT'}` — required for any public marketplace listing
evaluator-cookbook.md documented only 2 of 3 evaluator types (command, HTTP) — omitted the LLM judge entirely
AGENTS.md was missing the `analyze` subcommand and two source modules (llm_judge.py, spec_loader.py)
The .entire/ directory existed at project root but was not in .gitignore
CLAUDE.md is the most accurate reference doc in the project; AGENTS.md was the most stale
score_check.py is the only subprocess caller in the codebase without an explicit timeout — all other callers (command_evaluator, preflight, live_integration) pass timeout= consistently
test_doc_contract.py provides a documentation drift guard using regex to verify source keys exist in README

code.finding: Budget sentinel bug: `args.budget == 100` cannot distinguish user-explicit --budget 100 from argparse default; fixed by changing argparse default to None and adding fallback after spec merging
Redundant `except (ValueError, Exception)` — Exception is base class of ValueError; fixed to `except ValueError`
Code fence stripping logic inlined in _parse_judge_response duplicated _strip_code_fences (defined at line 290); fixed to call _strip_code_fences
Inconsistent error contracts: llm_judge_evaluator returns (0.0, {error}) tuples while analyze_for_dimensions raises RuntimeError — same module, incompatible patterns
Dual validation logic: validate_evaluator_payload (preflight) and _parse_evaluator_result (runtime) duplicate the same score-checking logic and can silently diverge
subprocess.run called without timeout= argument; added timeout=30 and subprocess.TimeoutExpired handler to prevent CI hang on misbehaving evaluator
--evaluator-command was appended before other flags, contradicting the documented nargs='+' greedy-parsing contract; moved to last position in cmd build
_normalize_quality_dimensions accepted duplicate dimension names silently, causing double-counting in _compute_weighted_score; added seen_names set check

workflow: Domain-specialized agent parallelization (5 agents each reading only their domain slice) produced higher-quality cross-cutting findings than file-by-file review would, especially for architectural issues
Ralph loop works best with machine-verifiable completion gates (pytest counts, file existence checks) rather than subjective criteria
Batching fixes into coherent groups (marketplace blockers → code correctness → tests → docs) with a test gate after each batch prevents regression accumulation
The ralph-loop plugin had a bug: ```! code blocks caused the shell to interpret <promise> tags in arguments; fix is to replace with Bash() function calls and use #!/usr/bin/env bash shebangs
PROMPT.md design for ralph-loops should include explicit 'What NOT to do' sections to prevent scope creep and risky refactors

friction: ralph-loop plugin was broken on first two launch attempts due to shell interpreting <promise> tags inside ```! code blocks — required reading a GitHub issue and patching the cached plugin files before the loop could start
Two API 500 internal server errors mid-session interrupted the initial workflow setup
Verifying the final test count required multiple bash command attempts — pytest -q output format made line counting non-trivial
The gepa mock path for the budget test required trial-and-error to discover the correct import path (gepa.optimize_anything.optimize_anything vs internal module path)
open_items: ~60 lines of evaluator-selection logic (3-way if/elif over evaluator_command/url/judge_model) still duplicated between _cmd_optimize and _cmd_score — a _build_evaluator() factory helper would centralize this
Intake loading pattern (_load_and_normalize_intake_spec + intake_requested sentinel) still repeated 3x in CLI command handlers
Evaluator generator templates (_generate_command_evaluator and _generate_http_evaluator) still copy-paste the dimension scoring logic — should be parameterized by transport mode
_load_spec_if_provided still returns dict | None | False (three-state) — non-idiomatic; should use typed exception or Result wrapper
Magic string '__optimize_anything_preflight__' still repeated without a module-level constant
score_check.py still hardcodes ['bash', evaluator_path] — constrains regression suite to bash-only evaluators

Several test coverage gaps remain: preflight timeout path, --intake-json + --intake-file mutual exclusion for optimize/score subcommands, non-finite score guard in simple mode, OSError branch in command_evaluator
Skills directories (skills/generate-evaluator/, skills/optimization-guide/) have no examples/ subdirectories — would improve skill concreteness for marketplace
plugin.json author field is a plain string; structured object with name+url would be better for marketplace indexing
